commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Codex app-server turns to keep OpenClaw runtime context out of `turn/start.input`, adds stale mirrored-history sanitization and thread reset behavior, and updates Codex prompt snapshots and tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current main prepends `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` to the Codex turn input, and the linked issue provides concrete logs showing those decorated user items replaying in later native Codex requests.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed for the Codex app-server protocol and prompt-boundary decision; this is not a safe automated repair lane while the replacement context channel is unsupported.

target.title: Keep Codex runtime context out of user history
target.body:
Fixes #84662.

## Summary

- stop prepending OpenClaw current-turn runtime context to the Codex `turn/start.input` user text
- pass that context through `collaborationMode.settings.reference_context` as non-authoritative reference data instead of `developer_instructions`
- sanitize stale mirrored Codex history entries that already contain old `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` user-message wrappers, and start a fresh native Codex thread when a bound thread would otherwise replay them
- update Codex prompt snapshot generation so MEMORY.md/current-turn context is no longer modeled as ordinary user input or developer instructions

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app-server turns should receive the actual user request as user input, while OpenClaw runtime/workspace context stays out of user history and out of developer-instruction authority. Existing mirrored histories with the old embedded runtime-context wrapper should not be replayed into a resumed native Codex thread.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in `/tmp/openclaw-pr-84790` on macOS, using the repository's Codex app-server runtime builders, mirrored session-history reader, Vitest app-server harness, prompt snapshot generator, and TypeScript test config.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-history.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `node /Users/andy/src/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --pretty false --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1`
- `node /Users/andy/src/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-history.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-history.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/protocol.ts test/helpers/agents/happy-path-prompt-snapshots.ts && git diff --check`
- `node --import /Users/andy/src/openclaw/node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`

Evidence after fix:

- Focused runtime tests passed: `Test Files 3 passed (3)`, `Tests 226 passed (226)`.
- Typecheck completed with exit code 0.
- Format/whitespace check completed with `All matched files use the correct format` and `git diff --check` clean.
- Prompt snapshot check completed with `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- The new stale-history runtime test builds a real mirrored session file containing `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` plus `Current user request: prior real request`, writes an existing native Codex binding, runs `runCodexAppServerAttempt`, and verifies no `thread/resume` request is sent, a fresh `thread/start` is sent, the `turn/start` input contains `prior real request` and `new request`, and the stale runtime-context marker is absent.
- The turn-lifecycle test verifies `turn/start.input` is exactly `user request only`, `collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` is `null`, and `collaborationMode.settings.reference_context` has `authority: "reference"` with the OpenClaw runtime context text.
- Regenerated prompt snapshots now show `reference_context` carrying the OpenClaw runtime context and show the developer-instructions layer empty for ordinary user turns.

Observed result after fix: Current-turn OpenClaw runtime context is no longer serialized into Codex user input or developer instructions, and stale history entries that already contain the old wrapper are stripped before mirrored history projection. If such stale history exists with a saved native thread binding, the app-server clears the binding and starts a new native Codex thread instead of resuming a thread that could replay the old privileged-looking user message.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Discord/Telegram production turn against the installed OpenClaw gateway from this PR branch. The proof above exercises the repository's Codex app-server runtime path locally, including the request payloads sent to the app-server client harness and the mirrored session-history reader.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-history.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `node /Users/andy/src/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --pretty false --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1`
- `node /Users/andy/src/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-history.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-history.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/protocol.ts test/helpers/agents/happy-path-prompt-snapshots.ts && git diff --check`
- `node --import /Users/andy/src/openclaw/node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`

If this PR is squash-merged or reworked, please preserve author attribution or include:

`Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>`
